data.body:
## Completion Verification ‚Äî PASS

Reviewed all six Acceptance Criteria against the implementation in `src/rebar/_snapshot/cache.py`, `src/rebar/_snapshot/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/_snapshot/repo_snapshot.py`, and `tests/unit/test_snapshot_cache.py`.

---

### AC1 ‚Äî Two concurrent gates requesting the same SHA trigger exactly ONE materialization (in-process single-flight)

**MET.**

`cache.py` maintains a `_sha_locks: dict[str, threading.Lock]` map (lines 55‚Äì65). `acquire()` takes `_sha_lock(sha)` before calling `materialize()` and re-checks `dest.is_dir()` after each lock acquisition (double-checked locking, lines 188‚Äì201). The test `test_in_process_single_flight` runs 4 threads, monkeypatches `_materialize_tree` with a counter, and asserts `len(builds) == 1`. A second test, `test_in_process_lock_collapses_without_flock`, pins the in-process lock in isolation (with `_interprocess_lock` replaced by `nullcontext`) and also asserts exactly one build.

---

### AC2 ‚Äî Two PROCESSES racing the same SHA collapse to one materialization (`flock(LOCK_EX)`)

**MET.**

`acquire()` calls `_interprocess_lock(_sha_lock_path(root, sha))` (line 192, `cache.py`), which is `_interprocess_lock` from `repo_snapshot.py` (lines 214‚Äì250) ‚Äî a `fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX)` with an atomic-mkdir spin-lock fallback. The test `test_cross_process_single_flight` spawns 3 real subprocesses competing for the same SHA, each writing to a shared marker file before sleeping, and asserts `builds == 1` after all processes complete.

---

### AC3 ‚Äî An entry removed while a reader holds it open does NOT disrupt the in-flight read (delete-on-last-close); a new lookup cleanly misses and re-materializes

**MET.**

`open_in_snapshot()` (lines 143‚Äì155, `cache.py`) opens a file and returns the fd; any `FileNotFoundError`/`OSError` on open raises `CacheMiss`. The test `test_open_reader_survives_eviction_then_relookup_misses` acquires an entry, opens a file fd, then does a janitor-style `os.rename(dest, trash)` + `shutil.rmtree(trash)`, reads the fd (asserts the content is still readable via POSIX delete-on-last-close), then calls `acquire()` again and asserts the entry is re-materialized clean.

---

### AC4 ‚Äî `ENOENT`/read-error on a cache entry is treated as a miss and self-healed by re-materialization

**MET.**

`open_in_snapshot()` translates `FileNotFoundError ‚Üí CacheMiss` and other `OSError ‚Üí CacheMiss` (lines 151‚Äì155). The `acquire()` loop re-materializes when `dest.is_dir()` is false (miss path at lines 188‚Äì201). Tests cover both paths: `test_open_in_snapshot_raises_cache_miss_on_enoent` asserts `CacheMiss` on missing file; `test_deleted_entry_self_heals` does `shutil.rmtree(dest)` on an existing entry then calls `acquire()` again and asserts the entry is recreated.

---

### AC5 ‚Äî Cache-hit recency is tracked by touch-on-read `mtime` (NOT `atime`); no PID/heartbeat lease exists

**MET.**

`touch_entry()` (lines 75‚Äì83) calls `os.utime(path, None)` ‚Äî `None` updates both atime and mtime to "now" using the OS wall clock, but the design doc is explicit that mtime is the recency signal consumed by the janitor. The `acquire()` hot-path calls `touch_entry(dest)` on every cache hit (lines 183, 190‚Äì191, 195‚Äì196) and never writes any PID. The test `test_cache_hit_touches_mtime` sets the entry's mtime to `now - 1000`, calls `acquire()` again, and asserts `entry_mtime(dest) > past + 100`. The test `test_no_pid_or_heartbeat_lease_in_module` reads `cache.__file__` source text and asserts neither `"getpid("` nor `"kill("` appears; confirmed via tool search: no `getpid` hit in the module.

---

### AC6 ‚Äî The byte-total increments atomically on populate (the value the janitor reconciles against)

**MET.**

`add_bytes()` (lines 109‚Äì125, `cache.py`) serializes under `_interprocess_lock(root / "locks" / "bytes.total.lock")`, reads the current total, adds `delta`, writes to a `.tmp` file, and atomically replaces with `os.replace()` ‚Äî no TOCTOU. `acquire()` calls `add_bytes(entry_size(dest), root)` exactly once per real populate (line 200), inside the cross-process flock, so concurrent populates cannot double-count. Tests: `test_byte_total_increments_on_populate` checks monotone increments after two distinct SHAs; `test_byte_total_not_double_counted_on_cache_hit` calls `acquire()` twice on the same SHA and asserts the total does not increase on the second call; `test_add_bytes_atomic_under_concurrency` runs 50 concurrent `add_bytes(+1)` calls and asserts the final total equals 50 (no lost update).

---

### File-impact obligations

All three files listed in the ticket's `file_impact` are present and non-empty:
- `src/rebar/_snapshot/cache.py` ‚Äî 201-line implementation
- `src/rebar/_snapshot/__init__.py` ‚Äî exports all cache symbols (`CacheMiss`, `acquire`, `add_bytes`, `byte_total`, `entry_mtime`, `entry_size`, `open_in_snapshot`, `touch_entry`)
- `tests/unit/test_snapshot_cache.py` ‚Äî 12 unit tests mapping to all 6 ACs

### Scope boundaries respected

- Eviction/janitor/disk reclamation: explicitly absent, correctly delegated to sibling S2b (b73c)
- Materialization: consumed via `repo_snapshot.materialize` (S1), not re-implemented

**Verdict: PASS. Every stated Acceptance Criterion is demonstrably implemented and covered by tests in the repository.**
